The First Puzzle

Elena ne neend nahi li thi. Uski apartment ek andhere toofan ke andar ki tarah dikhti thi — linguistics aur cryptography ke open books floor par bikheri hui, sticky notes half-solved patterns se chipki hui, laptop faintly buzzing kar raha tha jab software mysterious script ke against algorithms chala raha tha. Lekin ghanton ke baad bhi, parchment use taunt kar rahi thi. Kuch lines decrypt hui, baaki stubbornly resist kar rahi thi.

Use ek aur cup bitter coffee pi, aur apni aankhen khuli rakhi. Use ek cheez pakki pata chal gayi thi — agar message ne “Codex” aur “Guardians” ka zikr kiya hai, toh history ne sabko jhooth bola hai. Aur agar text ne “shadows” ka warning diya hai, toh jo aadmi ne kal raat threatening message bheja, woh bluff nahi kar raha tha.

Uski instincts kehti thi ki parchment complete nahi hai. Yeh kisi bada cheez ka ek fragment hai.

Subah tak, usne decide kar liya ki woh ek insaan se consult karegi — Professor Adrian Keller, uska purana mentor University of Vienna se. Ek aadmi jo medieval manuscripts ke obsession mein tha, aur jisne kabhi use kaha tha: “History apne truths ko lines ke beech chhupati hai. Trick yeh hai ki pata karo kahan dekhna hai.”

Usne parchment scan kiya, apni files encrypt kar li, aur last-minute flight book kar li. Har waqt, use ehsaas hota raha ki koi anjaan aankhen use dekh rahi hain. Airport par, use wahi dark sedan notice hua jo uske cab ke peeche trail kar raha tha. Jab usne shoulder ke upar dekha, toh driver ne apna chehra chhipa liya. Coincidence? Nahi. Ab woh sure thi — woh follow ki ja rahi thi.

Vienna ne use gray skies aur drizzle ke saath welcome kiya. Shahar ki cobblestones polished stone ki tarah chamak rahi thi, aur gothic spires clouds ko chhed rahi thi. Woh narrow streets se hote hue Keller ki office pahunchi — ek cramped chamber university archives ke andar chhupi hui.

Professor — sixties mein, graying beard, spectacles jo hamesha nose se fisal jaate the — ne use warmly greet kiya, lekin jaise hi usne parchment dikhaya, jam gaya.

Uske haath kaamp rahe the. “Tumhe yeh kahan se mila?”

“Mujhe mila. Courier ne deliver kiya. Na naam, na explanation.”

Keller ne apne glasses adjust kiye, symbols ko visible unease ke saath study kiya. “Kya tumhe pata hai ki tum kya pakde hue ho?”

“Main yehi umeed kar rahi thi ki aap mujhe bataenge.”

Usne awaz kam ki. “Yeh… yeh Scriptum Obscura jaisa lagta hai. Alexandria ki aag mein destroy ho gaya tha. Scholars kahte the ki fragments survive hue, ek secret brotherhood ne chhupa rakhe. Agar yeh genuine hai, Elena, toh tumne kuch aisa dhoondh liya jiske liye log marne tak ko taiyar rehte hain.”

Uska pet tight ho gaya. “The Codex?”

Keller ne slowly head nod kiya. “Codex ek single book nahi hai. Yeh ek collection hai — ek legacy. Jo log ise rakhte hain, woh narratives control karte hain, pasts rewrite karte hain, belief shape karte hain. Aur yeh fragment”—usne parchment ko tap kiya—“ek key hai.”

Jab tak Elena respond kar pati, corridor se ek crash ki awaz aayi. Dono ne tez se dekha.

Keller ka chehra fail gaya. “Hum akeli nahi hain.”

Office ka darwaza phoot kar khul gaya. Dark coats mein do aadmi andar ghus gaye, unki aankhen cold, movements precise. Ek ne Elena ki bag ki taraf lunga, woh instinctively peeche kheench li. Keller ne ek shelf shove ki, purani kitabein gir kar intruders ko thoda block kar diya.

“Run!” usne chillaya.

Elena side door se nikal gayi, parchment ko pakde hue. Woh twisting university halls mein daudti gayi, boots stone par goonj rahi thi. Peeche footsteps thunder kar rahe the. Woh courtyard mein nikal gayi, rain uske chehre par sting kar rahi thi.

Ek van screech karti hui nazar aayi. Ek terrifying pal ke liye, use laga yeh unka hi hai. Lekin sliding door khula, aur ek aurat ki awaz bulayi:

“Get in if you want to live!”

Elena ne hesitate kiya — lekin pursuers tezi se close ho rahe the. Bina koi choice ke, woh andar jump kar gayi. Van peel away hui, tires puddles mein splash karte hue.

Driver ek aurat thi Elena ki umar ki, dark hair back mein bandhi hui, eyes urgency se sharp. Usne khud ko introduce nahi kiya. Iske bajaye, use kaha, “Tumne abhi apne aap ko bahut valuable bana diya hai. Aur bahut dangerous.”

Elena ne parchment ko chest se pakda. “Tum kaun ho?”

Aurat ki nazar use flick ki. “Koi jisko tumhe marne nahi dena hai. Abhi tak.”

—

By nightfall, Elena shehar ke outskirts par ek hidden flat mein baithe thi. Mysterious woman — jo sirf apne aap ko Mira bola — ne parchment lamplight ke neeche spread kar diya.

“Tum lucky ho ki main Keller ki office par nazar rakh rahi thi,” Mira ne flatly kaha. “Warna Order tumhe ab tak pakad chuka hota.”

“The Order?” Elena ne poocha.

“Wohi shadows jinke baare mein tumhara text ne warning di thi. Inhone centuries se Codex ko protect kiya hai, aur outsiders ko tolerate nahi karte.”

Elena ne jhuk kar dekha. “Lekin kyun main? Kyun mujhe yeh bheja gaya?”

Mira ne hesitate kiya. Phir use finger se parchment par trace kiya aur woh cheez reveal ki jo Elena ne notice nahi ki thi: paper ke edge par faint notches.

“Yeh sirf writing nahi hai. Yeh ek puzzle ka hissa hai. Edges doosre fragments se align hote hain. Jo bhi ne yeh bheja hai, woh chahta hai ki tum map assemble karo.”

Elena ka dil tez dhadakne laga. Ek map. Ek path. Codex tak ki key.

Mira ki aankhen sankar hui. “Lekin yeh samajh lo — ek baar shuru kar diya, toh wapas nahi ja sakte. Log tumhe borders paar kar ke hunt karenge. Kuch tumhe marne chaheinge, kuch use karna chaheinge. Sawal yeh hai, Elena — kya tum sach jaanne ke liye itne desperate ho?”

Elena ne parchment ko dekha, us phrase ko jo abhi bhi uske dimaag mein goonj raha tha: The vanishing point is where truth begins.

Aur danger ke bawajood, fear ke bawajood, use apne resolve ko harden hota mehsoos hua.

“Haan,” use whisper kiya. “Main ise follow karungi.”

Bahar, lightning ne aasmaan ko chheer diya.

The Chase Begins

Rain safehouse ki narrow windows par takra rahi thi, uski rhythm andar ki khamoshi ko chhupa rahi thi. Elena parchment par jhuki baithe thi jabki Mira room mein caged panther ki tarah pace kar rahi thi. Dono auratein jaanti thi ki unke gird ke fragile walls jo aa raha hai, use rok nahi payenge. Order ne unhe pehle hi dhoondh liya tha; bas waqt ka sawal tha ki phir kab dhoondh lenge.

“Tumhara professor unhe slow karega,” Mira ne murmur kiya, jaise khud ko convince kar rahi ho. “Lekin woh tumhe chahenge, use nahi. Tum fragment carry kar rahi ho.”

Elena ne gehri saans li. Guilt uske chest mein khatk raha tha. Keller ka chehra — uski aankhen wide open jab intruders andar aaye — uske dimaag mein flash hua. Use chhod ke bhag gayi thi. Kya hua agar woh bahar nahi nikal paya? Kya use ne parchment dikhakar hi use doom kar diya?

Mira ne uski expression dekhi. “Regret par energy waste mat karo. Agar tum freeze ho gayi, toh tum mar gayi. Yeh unka advantage hai — woh tab strike karte hain jab tum hesitate karte ho.”

Elena ne use glare kiya. “Aur exactly tum kaun ho? Kahi se aake mujhe van mein kheench liya, aur claim kiya ki tum mujhe bacha rahe ho. Main tum par kyun bharosa karun?”

Mira ne smirk kiya, lekin woh uski aankhon tak nahi pahuncha. “Mat karo. Lekin ek cheez par bharosa karo — mere bina, tum ek ghante bhi nahi tikogi.”

Jab tak Elena respond kar pati, lights flicker hui. Flat mein low hum fail gaya, jaise storm se pehle ki static. Mira stiff ho gayi. “Unhone humein dhoondh liya.”

Pehla explosion building ko hila diya. Ceiling se dhool girne lagi. Mira ne Elena ka arm pakda. “Move!”

Woh back stairwell se daudne lagi jab upstairs glass shatter hua. Shadows safehouse mein pour hone lage — dark figures, masked, efficient. Elena ka chest heaving tha, lungs jal rahe the, lekin adrenaline use aage dhakel raha tha.

Woh rain se bhigi hui alley mein nikal gayi. Headlights snap hui — do black sedans street block kar rahe the. Doors khul gayi, aur long coats mein aadmi bahar aaye, unki formation military, purpose absolute.

Mira ne apne munh mein curse kiya. “Split!”

Use Elena ko side passage ki taraf dhakela aur opposite direction mein bolt kar gayi, apne peeche half pursuers kheenchte hue. Elena, heart hammering, alleys ke labyrinth mein force kar gayi, footsteps splash karte hue peeche se.

Left, phir right — uska dimaag blur tha. Ek haath ne uska shoulder pakda — use elbow se wildly swing kiya, kisi ke jaw par laga. Aadmi peeche lurch hua, ek second mila bhagne ke liye.

Uski instincts ne safe, crowded jagah ki talash ki. Woh ek bustling square mein nikal gayi, neon signs puddles mein reflect ho rahe the, log umbrellas ke neeche jaldi se chal rahe the. Ek pal ke liye, woh crowd mein mix ho gayi, saans lene ko gasping.

Phir use dekha.

Square ke对面, ek masked pursuer bilkul still khada tha, uski aankhen rain ke through use lock kar rahi thi. Woh chase nahi kar raha tha. Bas ek gloved hand uthaya aur temple ko touch kiya, jaise signal bhej raha ho.

Seconds mein, teen aur crowd se emerge hue.

Panic surge hua. Elena phir daud padi, startled pedestrians ke beech se weave karti hue. Ek child roya jab woh stroller se takra gayi. Woh awning ke neeche duck kar gayi, café ke through dash kiya, chairs giraye. Staff chillaya, lekin woh nahi ruki.

Dusri taraf, ek figure ne uska rasta rok diya.
